title: macOS: `podman machine start` never completes with `applehv` provider
body:
### Issue Description

Attempted to replace a working Podman machine on macOS after updating both OS and Podman. `podman machine init` completes successfully, but `podman machine start` never finishes.

During `podman machine start`, a vfkit window appears, with the contents entirely black. Activity monitor shows `Virtual Machine Service for vfkit` process using 100% CPU.

I have repeated this several times with the same results. Is this user error?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Remove old installation of `podman` and entire `~/.config/containers` directory.
2. `brew install podman`
3. `podman machine init --provider=applehv --cpus=8 -m=12 --disk-size=150`
4. `podman --log-level=debug machine start`


### Describe the results you received

```
zsh cfry@cannon ~ $ podman machine init --provider=applehv --cpus=8 -m=12 --disk-size=150

To start your machine run:

	podman machine start
```

During execution of `podman machine start`, a `vfkit` window appeared, containing only black.

### Describe the results you expected

Expected `podman machine start` to complete successfully without displaying a vfkit window.

### podman info output

```yaml
zsh cfry@cannon ~ $ podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       6.0.0
API Version:   6.0.0
Go Version:    go1.26.4
Built:         Wed Jun 24 07:28:09 2026
Build Origin:  brew
OS/Arch:       darwin/arm64
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 127.0.0.1:50943->127.0.0.1:50899: read: connection reset by peer
zsh cfry@cannon ~ $ podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
buildOrigin: brew
provider: libkrun
version: 6.0.0

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:50899: connect: connection refused
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Hardware: MacBook Pro 15", M1 Max CPU, 64 GB RAM, 2 TB SSD
OS: macOS Tahoe 26.5
Podman version: 6.0.0, installed via Homebrew
US Government owned computer, may have some additional S/W which is interfering with podman.
Running on USG VPN.


### Additional information

100% repeatable.

comments.body: please use the installer from our releases page.  that will help eliminate some problem areas. it's hard to say if this is user error, as you asked, but it definitely could be interference with other software.  i note you are using the `--provider` switch.  Does libkrun work (which is now the default) ?

> please use the installer from our releases page. that will help eliminate some problem areas.

I have been using Homebrew because I don't have admin privileges on this machine, and the installer from the GitHub releases page requires privileges because it will only install "for all users". I will ask our IT team for help.

BTW the link to the CLI installer from the `podman.io` front page is broken, it points to a "universal" installer which no longer exists.

> it's hard to say if this is user error, as you asked, but it definitely could be interference with other software. i note you are using the `--provider` switch. Does libkrun work (which is now the default) ?

No, it doesn't:

```

ERRO[0000] process 45420 has not ended

Error: exec: "krunkit": executable file not found in $PATH
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines                        
zsh cfry@cannon ~ $ 
```
Slightly off topic but looks like homebrew doesn't install krunkit by default as a dependency yet

I tried installing `krunkit` with the instructions from https://github.com/libkrun/krunkit but it had other issues. Possibly a version mismatch or additional user error? Waiting on our IT folks to run the official installer.

Our IT folks installed `podman` from the official package into `/opt/podman`. 

Still no luck getting the machine started. `podman machine init --cpus=8 -m=12 --disk-size=150` works as before. But `podman machine start` doesn't terminate.

When using `--log-level=debug`, a new Terminal tab opens up and displays:
```
Last login: Mon Jun 29 14:24:27 on ttys002
/var/folders/k8/22mzz8gj1n30hwprkpgfnnfh0000gw/T/podman/krunkit-debug.sh ; exit;
zsh cfry@cannon ~ $ /var/folders/k8/22mzz8gj1n30hwprkpgfnnfh0000gw/T/podman/krunkit-debug.sh ; exit;
```

control-C aborts the `podman` command but `krunkit` continues running at 100% CPU. `podman machine stop` hangs. `krunkit` continues running until killed.

Tried again, creating the machine with `--provider=applehv`. Same behavior as before: `Virtual Machine Service for vfkit` process runs at 100% until the `start` command is aborted with control-C. `podman machine stop` completes.

I'll take a SWAG that this machine has an issue with the Linux VM image.

`-m 12` will start a VM with 12 MiB of RAM, I’m guessing you mean `-m 12000` or so.

```
-m, --memory uint            Memory in MiB (default 2048)
```

We could add an error when running `podman init` if the memory assigned to the VM is less than 1024 MiB or such. `-m 512` hangs on my m1, `-m 1024` started fine.

> `-m 12` will start a VM with 12 MiB of RAM, I’m guessing you mean `-m 12000` or so.

D'OH!! So it was (largely) user error on my part.

`podman machine init --provider=applehv --cpus=8 -m=12288 --disk-size=150` followed by `podman machine start` works as expected.

I'd file a report with Homebrew for the missing dependencies, but this is a non-standard installation, so they're likely to ignore it.

Thanks for the help, and sorry for the confusion.
